panels.description: Wide, somber establishing shot. Focus on the interior of a grand but dimly lit mansion hall shortly after a funeral. Dust motes drift in shafts of weak morning light. The atmosphere is heavy with unspoken grief. No characters visible, emphasizing the void.

bubbles.text: I need not describe the feelings of those whose dearest ties are rent by that most irreparable evil, the void that presents itself to the soul, and the despair that is exhibited on the countenance.
It is so long before the mind can persuade itself that she whom we saw every day... can be hushed, never more to be heard.

panels.description: Medium close-up on 'I' (The Protagonist), sitting alone in a dark study chair, head bowed, hands clasped tightly over his knee. Strong chiaroscuro lighting emphasizes his inner turmoil. His expression is one of deep contemplation and shared misery.

bubbles.text: These are the reflections of the first days; but when the lapse of time proves the reality of the evil, then the actual bitterness of grief commences.
Yet from whom has not that rude hand rent away some dear connection? And why should I describe a sorrow which all have felt, and must feel?

panels.description: Medium shot focusing on 'my father' and 'I' standing near a window, looking outwards but distracted. The father places a comforting, tired hand on the protagonist's shoulder. Soft light breaking through the clouds outside suggests a slight shift towards endurance.

bubbles.text: The time at length arrives when grief is rather an indulgence than a necessity; and the smile that plays upon the lips, although it may be deemed a sacrilege, is not banished.

My mother was dead, but we had still duties which we ought to perform; we must continue our course with the rest and learn to think ourselves fortunate whilst one remains whom the spoiler has not seized.

panels.description: Medium close-up of the protagonist, face partially obscured by shadow, looking down at papers or a letter (perhaps confirming the departure date). His posture is rigid, indicating reluctance and inner conflict about leaving the safety of mourning.

bubbles.text: My departure for Ingolstadt... was now again determined upon. I obtained from my father a respite of some weeks.
It appeared to me sacrilege so soon to leave the repose, akin to death, of the house of mourning and to rush into the thick of life.

panels.description: Medium shot: Focus on Elizabeth. She is dressed simply but elegantly, offering a tentative, comforting smile towards the protagonist (who is slightly out of frame or just visible). Soft, hopeful light illuminates her face, contrasting the previous darkness.

bubbles.text: I was unwilling to quit the sight of those that remained to me... She indeed veiled her grief and strove to act the comforter to us all. She looked steadily on life and assumed its duties with courage and zeal.

panels.description: Wide panel spanning the bottom edge, focused on the previous evening. A silhouette of 'I' and Clerval leaning against each other in deep conversation by a flickering lamp. Clerval looks frustrated, while the Protagonist listens intently. Focus is on shared, frustrated ambition.

bubbles.text: The day of my departure at length arrived. Clerval spent the last evening with us... Henry deeply felt the misfortune of being debarred from a liberal education.
